name: Semantic Code Search
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: Code understanding and embedding generation — text-embedding-3-large for semantic code vectors, GPT-4o-mini for natural-language-to-code query translation, code summarization, and search result explanation with contextual code snippets

dev.note: ~40K tokens/day, embedding generation and query testing

services.name: Azure AI Search
services.purpose: Code search index — hybrid search combining keyword matching (function names, class names, identifiers) with vector similarity for semantic queries ("find the retry logic", "where is authentication handled"); faceted search by language, repository, and file type

services.name: Azure Blob Storage
services.purpose: Code repository mirror and chunk storage — cloned repository snapshots, parsed AST (Abstract Syntax Tree) outputs, pre-computed embeddings, indexing pipeline artifacts, and search result cache for popular queries

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Search platform runtime — hosts the search API, code parsing and chunking workers, incremental indexing pipeline, query processing service, and developer-facing search UI backend

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: OpenAI API keys, search service keys, Git repository access tokens (PAT/SSH), storage account keys, and webhook signing secrets

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Search platform observability — query latency (P50/P95), search relevance metrics (MRR, NDCG), indexing pipeline throughput, embedding generation rate, and developer usage analytics

optimization_tips: Use text-embedding-3-large with reduced dimensions (1024 instead of 3072) — achieves 95% of full-dimension retrieval quality at 1/3 the storage and vector search cost in AI Search
Implement incremental indexing via Git webhooks — only re-index changed files on push events instead of full repository re-scans, reducing embedding generation costs by 90% for active repositories

Use GPT-4o-mini for query expansion and result summarization instead of GPT-4o — code search queries are typically short and structured, making mini models sufficient for query understanding at 1/30th the cost

Cache popular search queries and their results with a 1-hour TTL — developer search patterns follow a power law where 20% of queries account for 80% of volume, significantly reducing AI Search and embedding costs

Chunk code at function/class granularity using AST parsing instead of fixed-size text chunks — produces more meaningful search results and reduces the number of chunks by 40-60% compared to line-based splitting
Use AI Search Free tier for dev environments (50MB index) — sufficient for indexing a single small-to-medium codebase during development without any search service cost
